title: Mistral AI Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Mistral AI** is a hosted LLM provider exposing a stateless REST API under `https://api.mistral.ai/v1`. This connector — `MistralConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "mistral"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) — wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs from Mistral:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Chat Completions | `/chat/completions` | LLM chat generation (sync + streaming) |
| Embeddings | `/embeddings` | Vector embeddings for retrieval |
| Models | `/models` | List, get, delete fine-tuned models |
| Files | `/files` | Upload + manage training / batch corpora |
| Fine-tuning | `/fine_tuning/jobs` | Launch + monitor fine-tune runs |

The connector normalises models + uploaded files + fine-tuning jobs into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), surfaces standalone `async def` methods per user-requested operation (OCP), retries 429/5xx with exponential backoff + jitter (honouring `Retry-After`), and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC — all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::MistralHTTPClient`).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Generate a Mistral API key

1. Sign in to https://console.mistral.ai.
2. Open **API Keys** in the left nav.
3. Click **Create new key**, name it (e.g. `shielva-prod`), and copy the value — Mistral shows it only once.

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → Mistral AI**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `api_key` (secret) | API key from step 3 |
| `base_url` (text, optional) | Override `https://api.mistral.ai/v1` (rarely needed) |
| `default_chat_model` (text, optional) | Hint for callers, default `mistral-large-latest` |
| `default_embed_model` (text, optional) | Hint for callers, default `mistral-embed` |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number) | Soft cap, default 60 |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates `api_key` is present, probes `GET /models` once to confirm the key is accepted, initialises the `MistralHTTPClient`, and returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, CONNECTED)`.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which lists models as a lightweight probe. On 2xx → `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401 → `DEGRADED + TOKEN_EXPIRED`. On network failure → `OFFLINE + CONNECTED`.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Mistral REST API uses **Bearer API key authentication** (`AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`). No OAuth dance, no token refresh, no expiry.

### Credential model

- `api_key` is sent in the `Authorization` header as `Bearer <api_key>`.
- For `POST /files` the request is `multipart/form-data`; httpx sets `Content-Type` automatically when `files=` is used, so the client only sets `Authorization` + `Accept`.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `api_key` is present, probes `GET /models` once. Returns `HEALTHY/CONNECTED` on 2xx; `DEGRADED/MISSING_CREDENTIALS` on 401; `DEGRADED/CONNECTED` on transient network failure (installed but probe failed). |
| `authorize(...)` | Returns a synthetic `TokenInfo(access_token=api_key, token_type="Bearer")` — no token exchange. |
| `health_check()` | `GET /models` — minimal probe. |
| `ensure_token()` | Not called — no token to refresh. |
| token storage | None. |

### Header contract

Every outbound JSON request:

```
Authorization: Bearer <api_key>
Content-Type:  application/json
Accept:        application/json
```

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `api_key` | secret | yes | `self.api_key` | Bearer token sent on every request |
| `base_url` | text | no | `self.base_url` (defaults to `https://api.mistral.ai/v1`) | Override for proxy / sandbox |
| `default_chat_model` | text | no | `self.default_chat_model` (default `mistral-large-latest`) | Caller hint |
| `default_embed_model` | text | no | `self.default_embed_model` (default `mistral-embed`) | Caller hint |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 60) | Client-side soft cap |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["api_key"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("DEGRADED",  "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
_MISTRAL_BASE = "https://api.mistral.ai/v1"
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `MistralConnector`. SOC: connector.py orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::MistralHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `api_key`; persist config; probe `GET /models` once.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
No-op for api_key auth — returns a synthetic TokenInfo wrapping `api_key` as `Bearer`.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Lists models via `GET /models`. Classifies failures by `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Catalogues models + uploaded files → `NormalizedDocument` → `ingest_document`. Returns `SyncResult` with counts. Mistral has no real-time syncable corpus.

children.title: Inference (Chat + Embeddings)
children.content:
### `async create_chat_completion(model, messages, *, temperature=0.7, max_tokens=1024, top_p=1.0, stream=False, response_format=None, tools=None) -> Dict`
`POST /chat/completions` — body built by `helpers.utils.build_chat_payload`. Optional fields (`response_format`, `tools`) are omitted when `None`.

### `async create_embeddings(model, inputs, *, encoding_format="float") -> Dict`
`POST /embeddings` — body: `{model, input: inputs, encoding_format}`. Note Mistral wire key is `input` (singular) even though it accepts a list.

children.title: Models
children.content:
### `async list_models() -> Dict`
`GET /models`.

### `async get_model(model_id) -> Dict`
`GET /models/{model_id}`.

### `async delete_model(model_id) -> Dict`
`DELETE /models/{model_id}` — only fine-tuned models are deletable.

children.title: Files
children.content:
### `async list_files(*, purpose=None, page=0, page_size=100) -> Dict`
`GET /files?page=N&page_size=M&purpose=...`.

### `async upload_file(purpose, file_path) -> Dict`
`POST /files` — multipart upload: `file=<bytes>`, `purpose=<string>`.

### `async get_file(file_id) -> Dict`
`GET /files/{file_id}`.

### `async delete_file(file_id) -> Dict`
`DELETE /files/{file_id}`.

children.title: Fine-tuning
children.content:
### `async list_fine_tuning_jobs(*, page=0, page_size=100) -> Dict`
`GET /fine_tuning/jobs`.

### `async create_fine_tuning_job(model, training_files, *, hyperparameters=None) -> Dict`
`POST /fine_tuning/jobs` — body: `{model, training_files: [{file_id}], hyperparameters}`. `training_files` may be a list of file_id strings (auto-wrapped) or already-built `{file_id}` dicts.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
MistralError                            # base; carries status_code + response_body
├── MistralAuthError                    # 401 / 403
├── MistralBadRequestError              # 400
├── MistralNotFoundError                # 404
├── MistralRateLimitError               # 429 — retry_after_s honoured by retry loop
└── MistralServerError                  # 5xx
```

Back-compat aliases preserved:
```
MistralNetworkError = MistralServerError
MistralNotFound     = MistralNotFoundError
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request_with_retry`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 | Raise immediately |
| 429 | Honour `Retry-After`; otherwise exponential backoff `_BACKOFF_BASE * 2 ** attempt + jitter(0, 0.25)`, retry up to `_MAX_RETRIES=3` |
| 5xx | Same exponential backoff + jitter, retry up to 3 |
| `httpx.TimeoutException` / `httpx.NetworkError` | Backoff, retry up to 3, then raise `MistralNetworkError` |

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  TOKEN_EXPIRED)
403 → ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
429 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)   — logged as warning
network → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, CONNECTED)
other → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `api_key` blank | Re-enter the key in ACP. Mistral shows the key only once at generation — rotate if lost. |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | 401/403 from Mistral — key invalid or revoked | Verify the key in console.mistral.ai → API Keys. Rotate if needed. |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, TOKEN_EXPIRED)` at health_check | 401 — API key revoked | Rotate the key. |
| `mistral.http.retry` warnings spike | Hitting 429 (Mistral per-minute rate cap) | The client retries with exponential backoff + `Retry-After`. Lower `rate_limit_per_min` or batch fewer requests. |
| `MistralBadRequestError` on `create_chat_completion` | Model rejected the payload — e.g. unsupported `response_format`, oversize `messages` | Re-read the API docs; tools/response_format support varies per model. |
| `MistralError: File not found` from `upload_file` | Local `file_path` is wrong or unreadable | Verify the path; ensure the process has read permission. |
| `ImportError: cannot import name 'MistralNotFound'` | Older exceptions.py without back-compat alias | Already fixed — `MistralNotFound = MistralNotFoundError` alias added. |
